codeReview.summary: The feature meets its contract: the commit adds a dedicated deployment/proof script, deploys CrossChainQuoteEstimator on Polkadot Hub TestNet, successfully calls `estimateCrossChainQuoteCost` through the deployed contract, and records a non-zero `weighMessage` result in `polkadot-hub-testnet-xcm-proof.json` (`refTime=979880000`, `proofSize=10943`). I found one non-blocking robustness issue in the fallback limitation logic.

issues.description: The failure path treats an empty `eth_getCode` result as proof that the XCM precompile is unavailable on the REVM route and writes that conclusion into `knownLimitation`. That inference is brittle for precompiles, because EVM precompiles can be callable even when `getCode()` returns empty. The current proof run succeeded, so this did not break the delivered artifact, but on a future failing run the script could misclassify an unknown revert as a confirmed network limitation instead of recording the actual uncertainty.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The shared XCM precompile note has not been updated with the newly proven live-testnet behavior. Future workers reading only the library will still think the testnet capability is unconfirmed and may repeat this investigation.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `/home/kpa/polkadot/.factory/library/xcm-precompile.md:23-26` still says live `weighMessage` behavior 'must be validated on Polkadot Hub TestNet'. This feature's proof artifact at `/home/kpa/polkadot/dualvm/deployments/polkadot-hub-testnet-xcm-proof.json` records a successful live call with `xcmPrecompileHasCode=true`, `refTime=979880000`, and `proofSize=10943`, and the handoff `/home/kpa/.factory/missions/dc664f26-9086-4d9b-896d-ba8f3bde8d71/handoffs/2026-03-17T01-21-03-200Z__xcm-precompile-live-test__b2051f58-d16a-446d-b7e7-6cba95507e14.json` repeats the same result.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The deployment-worker skill should explicitly tell workers how to validate `.env` presence/configuration without printing secret material. In this run the worker inspected `.env` by grepping `PRIVATE_KEY=` and echoed part of the secret into the transcript.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `/home/kpa/polkadot/.factory/skills/deployment-worker/SKILL.md:24-28` requires checking that `.env` exists with required private keys, but does not warn against echoing key values. The transcript skeleton for worker session `b2051f58-d16a-446d-b7e7-6cba95507e14` in `/home/kpa/.factory/missions/dc664f26-9086-4d9b-896d-ba8f3bde8d71/worker-transcripts.jsonl` shows the command `grep "PRIVATE_KEY=" .env | head -1 | cut -c1-20` and the output `PRIVATE_KEY=0x0f4a62`.

sharedStateObservations.observation: A recurring deployment helper command used by the deployment skill is missing from the shared services/commands registry, so workers keep invoking it ad hoc.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: The deployment-worker skill at `/home/kpa/polkadot/.factory/skills/deployment-worker/SKILL.md:24-28` tells workers to run `cd /home/kpa/polkadot/dualvm && node scripts/check-testnet-balance.mjs`, and this feature's handoff/transcript used that command to confirm funding. `/home/kpa/polkadot/.factory/services.yaml` defines install/test/typecheck/build/compile commands but has no reusable balance-check command entry.

summary: Pass. I reviewed the feature metadata, handoff, transcript skeleton, commit diff, deployment proof JSON, and supporting XCM contract/test files. The live proof is credible and satisfies VAL-XCM-001; the only code issue I found is a non-blocking weakness in how the script diagnoses the unavailable-precompile fallback path.
